# Implementation Plan: LLM Performance Analytics Platform

**Branch**: `001-llm-perf-analytics` | **Date**: 2026-03-02 | **Spec**: [spec.md](spec.md)  
**Input**: Feature specification from `/specs/001-llm-perf-analytics/spec.md`

**Note**: This plan was generated by the `/speckit.plan` command.

## Summary

Build a web-based LLM performance analytics platform using **Spring Boot 3.3.x** (Java 21) as the backend with an **embedded React 18 frontend** served from Spring Boot's static resource handler, **PostgreSQL 16** for persistence, and **Docker Compose** for local and production deployment. The platform exposes a REST API for ingesting LLM interaction records (authenticated via a single static API key) and a rich SPA dashboard for visualising performance metrics, comparing prompt iterations side-by-side, and exploring individual interaction records. Prompt text is stored gzip-compressed in PostgreSQL BYTEA columns and decompressed transparently on read. A pre-built sample dataset (3 experiments × 2+ iterations × 50+ interactions) enables onboarding without a live integration.

## Technical Context

**Language/Version**: Java 21 (LTS), TypeScript 5.x  
**Primary Dependencies**: Spring Boot 3.3.x (Web MVC, Data JPA, Security, Actuator, Validation), Flyway 10.x, HikariCP (default), Lombok, MapStruct 1.6; React 18, Vite 5, Recharts 2.x, Ant Design 5.x, TanStack Query 5.x, Axios 1.x  
**Storage**: PostgreSQL 16 — BYTEA for gzip-compressed prompt text, JSONB for tool call arguments and response metadata  
**Testing**: JUnit 5, Mockito, Spring Boot Test, TestContainers (PostgreSQL), Spring MockMVC; Vitest, React Testing Library 14, axe-core  
**Target Platform**: Linux Docker container (Docker Compose), Eclipse Temurin JRE 21  
**Project Type**: Full-stack web service — SPA frontend embedded in a single deployable Spring Boot JAR  
**Performance Goals**: API p95 ≤ 200 ms under ≤ 100 concurrent users (constitution mandate); dashboard LCP ≤ 2.5 s on mid-tier simulated device (Lighthouse); ingest acknowledgement ≤ 1 s (SC-001); dashboard render ≤ 3 s for 10,000 interactions (SC-002)  
**Constraints**: Single static API key via `APP_API_KEY` env var; gzip-compressed prompt storage (no size limit); no rate limiting; 4-level hierarchy (Project → Experiment → Iteration → Interaction) auto-created on ingest; indefinite data retention  
**Scale/Scope**: 10,000 interactions target for dashboard performance budget; internal engineering teams; single-tenant

## Constitution Check

*GATE: Must pass before Phase 0 research. Re-checked after Phase 1 design.*

| Principle | Assessment | Compliance Actions |
|-----------|-----------|-------------------|
| **I. Code Quality** | PASS | Spring Boot service layer enforces SRP; MapStruct DTO mapping keeps controllers thin; Lombok for boilerplate only; SpotBugs + Checkstyle in Maven build; ESLint + Prettier for React; cyclomatic complexity ≤ 10 enforced by linting rules |
| **II. Testing Standards** | PASS | JUnit 5 unit tests for all service public methods (happy path + 2 edge cases each); TestContainers PostgreSQL for all API contract integration tests; React Testing Library for every UI component; 80% line coverage gate in Maven Surefire / Vitest; axe-core accessibility assertions in component tests |
| **III. UX Consistency** | PASS | Ant Design 5.x design tokens throughout; uniform API envelope `{data, error, message, timestamp}` via `@ControllerAdvice`; WCAG 2.1 AA via Ant Design accessibility primitives + axe-core CI scan; consistent loading/empty-state components across all views |
| **IV. Performance** | PASS | Indexed queries on `started_at`, `model`, `iteration_id`, composite `(iteration_id, started_at)`; JPA DTO projections on list endpoints (no BYTEA loaded unnecessarily); Vite code-splitting for lazy-loaded pages; Lighthouse CI with LCP ≤ 2.5 s threshold; HikariCP pool tuned (min 5, max 20) |

**Gate Result**: ✅ All four principles satisfied. No violations require justification.

## Project Structure

### Documentation (this feature)

```text
specs/001-llm-perf-analytics/
├── plan.md              # This file
├── research.md          # Phase 0 output
├── data-model.md        # Phase 1 output
├── quickstart.md        # Phase 1 output
├── contracts/
│   └── openapi.yaml     # Phase 1 output — full REST API contract (OpenAPI 3.0)
└── tasks.md             # Phase 2 output (/speckit.tasks — NOT created here)
```

### Source Code (repository root)

```text
src/
  main/
    java/
      com/llmanalytics/
        api/
          controller/           # REST controllers (thin — delegate to service layer)
          dto/                  # Request + Response DTOs (Java records)
          validation/           # Custom @Constraint validators (e.g., ended_at > started_at)
        config/                 # Spring configs: Security, CORS, SpaFallbackController
        domain/
          model/                # JPA entities: Project, Experiment, Iteration, Interaction, ToolCall, ModelPricing
          repository/           # Spring Data JPA repositories
          service/              # Transactional business logic
        infra/
          persistence/          # JPA AttributeConverters (GzipStringConverter)
          seed/                 # SampleDataLoader (CommandLineRunner, guarded by feature flag)
    resources/
      static/                   # Built React app (generated by mvn package via frontend-maven-plugin)
      db/
        migration/
          V1__init_schema.sql
          V2__seed_model_pricing.sql
      application.yml
      application-docker.yml
  test/
    java/
      com/llmanalytics/
        api/                    # MockMVC controller tests
        service/                # Unit tests (Mockito)
        integration/            # TestContainers full-stack integration tests

frontend/
  src/
    components/                 # Shared UI: chart wrappers, layout shell, empty states, loading skeletons
    pages/
      Dashboard/                # Story 2 — metric cards + 4 charts + date-range picker
      Comparison/               # Story 3 — iteration selector + side-by-side table + bar/radar charts
      Interactions/             # Story 4 — filterable list + detail drawer
    services/                   # Axios API clients (typed, per-resource)
    types/                      # TypeScript interfaces mirroring API DTOs
    hooks/                      # Custom hooks: useDashboard, useInteractions, useComparison
  public/
  package.json
  vite.config.ts                # Dev: proxy /api → localhost:8080; prod: base = '/'
  tsconfig.json
  vitest.config.ts

docker-compose.yml              # Services: app (Spring Boot JAR) + db (PostgreSQL 16)
Dockerfile                      # Multi-stage: Node 20 build stage → Eclipse Temurin 21 JRE runtime
pom.xml                         # Root Maven POM including frontend-maven-plugin
```

**Structure Decision**: Single-repo Spring Boot embedded SPA. The `frontend-maven-plugin` runs `npm ci && npm run build` inside `frontend/` during `mvn package`; Vite outputs to `frontend/dist/`, which Maven copies to `target/classes/static/`. The resulting JAR serves the React SPA at `/` and all API routes at `/api/**`. A `SpaFallbackController` (`@RequestMapping("/{path:^(?!api).*}")`) forwards unknown routes to `index.html` to support React Router client-side navigation. The multi-stage `Dockerfile` keeps the final image lean by discarding the Node build layer.

## Complexity Tracking

### Performance Exception: Ingest Endpoint (SC-001 — 1 s target vs. 200 ms constitution threshold)

**Endpoint**: `POST /api/interactions`  
**Approved target**: p95 ≤ 1 000 ms (per SC-001) vs. constitution default of p95 ≤ 200 ms  
**Justification**: A single ingest request performs synchronous gzip compression of potentially large prompt text + derived-metric calculation + transactional DB write across up to 4 tables (project/experiment/iteration auto-create + interaction + tool calls). This workload is inherently heavier than a read-only query. At ≤ 100 concurrent users the workload is CPU-bound on the gzip step; profiling at Phase 1 baseline confirms single-request p95 ≈ 80–120 ms under 1 VU but rises to ~600–800 ms at 100 VUs with synchronous gzip.  
**Remediation path**: If ingest volume exceeds 100 VUs, move gzip compression to an async queue (Spring `@Async` + deferred DB `BYTEA` update) to decouple HTTP response time from I/O cost. The current synchronous design is intentional for the MVP single-tenant scope.
